channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Scarves over headscarves, Muslim women’s outdoors group tackles snow tubing in Minnesota
item.description: Three years ago, a Somali-American mom in Minneapolis founded a group for Muslim women who want to enjoy the great outdoors.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former FBI informant will be sentenced for bogus bribery claim about President Biden and son Hunter
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors will ask a judge on Wednesday for six years in prison for a former FBI informant whose bogus story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes became central to Republicans' impeachment effort.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill at least 5 in Gaza, including 2 babies, Palestinians say
item.description: Israeli airstrikes killed at least five people in the Gaza Strip, including two infants and a woman, Palestinian medics said Wednesday. One strike hit a home in the central city of Deir al-Balah, killing two men and a woman, according to Al-Aqsa Martyrs Hospital, which received the casualties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LIVE: Thousands flee as wildfires burn out of control in and around Los Angeles and homes are destroyed
item.description: California firefighters battled wind-whipped wildfires that tore across the Los Angeles area, destroying homes, clogging roadways as tens of thousands fled and straining resources as the fires burned uncontained early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JULIE WATSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former St Louis prosecutor Kim Gardner spent hundreds of work hours on nursing degree, scathing audit finds
item.description: Report released by Missouri State Auditor Scott Fitzpatrick Tuesday details what an investigation found during former Democratic St. Louis Circuit Attorney Kim Gardner's tenure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter continues to lie in state at Capitol Rotunda ahead of his state funeral
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will continue to lie in state Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Colleges prepare for new legal and political terrain under Trump
item.description: Colleges and universities are preparing for new legal and political territory as President-elect Trump returns to office. Concerns for international students, GOP attacks on higher education and increased protests on campus are just some of the worries on administrators' minds.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Carter and Biden's long friendship had wrinkles. It will be on display a final time with a eulogy
item.description: Joe Biden and Jimmy Carter's decades-long friendship will be on display one last time as Biden eulogizes Carter during his state funeral at Washington National Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow And Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean court clears government, adoption agency of liability in adoptee's deportation from US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court on Wednesday cleared the government and an adoption agency of all liability in a lawsuit filed by a 49-year-old Korean man whose traumatic adoption journey led to an abusive childhood in the United States and ultimately his deportation to South Korea in
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's rolls back diversity goals
item.description: Fast food giant McDonald’s has become the latest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) commitments following the Supreme Court’s 2023 decision ending affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Southern US shivers as new winter storm threatens snow for Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — A developing winter storm threatens to drop snow, sleet and freezing rain on parts of Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas as frigid air that escaped the Arctic plunges temperatures to subfreezing levels in some of the southernmost points of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's woodworking, painting and poetry reveal an introspective Renaissance Man
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — The world knew Jimmy Carter as a president and humanitarian, but he also was a woodworker, painter and poet, creating a body of artistic work that reflects deeply personal views of the global community — and himself.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Wildfire burns through LA enclave known for its beaches and celebrity allure
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rimmed to the south by a 3-mile stretch of Pacific beach, and to the north by craggy bluffs and deep, verdant canyons, the seaside enclave of Pacific Palisades has long been a celebrity hideaway and magnet for those drawn to nature’s doorstep not far from the skyline of downtown L
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, WGN 9

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who exploded Tesla Cybertruck outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI, police say
item.description: The highly decorated soldier who exploded a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI including ChatGPT to help plan the attack, Las Vegas police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawyers collecting $148 million judgment from Rudy Giuliani say World Series rings must be given up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for two former Georgia election workers who are owed $148 million in damages after suing Rudy Giuliani for defamation said Tuesday that evidence proves their clients are entitled to three World Series rings that the former New York City mayor says he gave to his son.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schools around the US are weighing responses to possible immigration raids
item.description: Schools around the country are reviewing what to do if immigration agents come knocking as President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office after campaigning on a pledge to deport millions of people.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials use bulldozer to shove abandoned vehicles out of the way near Los Angeles fire
item.description: Drivers who abandoned their vehicles fleeing the growing fire in Los Angeles might find their cars a little worse for wear when they return.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KRON4

item.title: As MLK holiday nears, Bernice King urges people to really focus on his teachings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — As the holiday honoring slain civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. approaches, his daughter is urging people to really focus on his teachings and work throughout the coming year, rather than just quoting him or celebrating for a day. The Rev.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Woman accused of drunk driving said police arrested her over Trump vote
item.description: The 61-year-old woman also called her arresting officer "Lucifer" and accused him of being part of "the Deep State.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: New York appeals court denies halting Trump's sentencing
item.description: A New York appellate judge on Tuesday declined to suspend President-elect Trump’s Friday sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Payments go out in $100M Verizon settlement, but people are disappointed
item.description: Payments have finally started going out in the $100 million class action lawsuit Verizon agreed to settle, but the deposits are falling short of people's expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Peter Yarrow of Peter, Paul and Mary dies at 86
item.description: Yarrow, who also co-wrote the group's most enduring song, “Puff the Magic Dragon,” died Tuesday in New York.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: “Look, the Panama Canal is vital to our country, it’s being operated by China, China. And we gave the Panama Canal to Panama, we didn’t give it to China,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: New labels to help identify devices less at risk of hacking
item.description: The federal government is rolling out a consumer labeling system designed to help Americans pick smart devices that are less vulnerable to hacking.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rapper Trae tha Truth reunited with daughter after abduction
item.description: On New Year’s Day, Trae received the news he had been praying for: Truth was found safe near the Mexican border in California.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KRON4

item.title: 3-year-old boy dies from Honolulu fireworks explosion injuries, raising toll to 4
item.description: The U.S. military flew six of the injured to Phoenix for treatment on Saturday because Hawaii’s lone burn unit doesn’t have enough capacity to care for all the victims.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US transfers 11 Guantanamo detainees to Oman after more than two decades without charge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon said Monday it had transferred 11 Yemeni men to Oman this week after holding them for more than two decades without charge at the U.S. naval base in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Immigration is a higher priority for Americans than it was a year ago, an AP-NORC poll shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More Americans say immigration should be a top focus for the U.S. government in 2025, as the country heads toward a new Republican administration in which President-elect Donald Trump has promised the mass deportations of migrants and an end to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, HUMERA LODHI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, church leader asks for prayers for victims of truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — At a Mass celebrated Sunday at New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, just blocks from where a man sped down Bourbon Street in a deadly attack early on New Year's Day, a church leader acknowledged the sense of unease that remains and asked for prayers for victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, Fox 5
